{

  "concepts": [
    { "id": "c-generative-ai", "name": "Generative AI", "aliases": ["Generative Artificial Intelligence", "AI"], "description": "Generative artificial intelligence systems that intermediate how the public consumes public statistical information.", "grounding_span": "Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) is redefining how people interact with public information", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "id": "c-llm", "name": "Large language models", "aliases": ["LLMs"], "description": "Language models that draw on public datasets to answer user queries.", "grounding_span": "Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) mean that more Americans are getting answers from AI chatbots", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "id": "c-ai-chatbot", "name": "AI chatbots", "aliases": ["AI systems", "AI assistants"], "description": "Conversational AI systems through which Americans increasingly obtain answers drawn from public datasets.", "grounding_span": "more Americans are getting answers from AI chatbots and other AI systems", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "id": "c-public-data", "name": "Public data", "aliases": ["public datasets", "public information"], "description": "Publicly available data whose consumption is being reshaped by generative AI.", "grounding_span": "shaping how public data are consumed", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "id": "c-federal-statistics", "name": "Federal statistics", "aliases": ["official statistics", "federal statistical data"], "description": "Official federal statistical products that agencies seek to keep front-and-center in AI-driven experiences.", "grounding_span": "advance the use of federal statistics with generative AI to ensure that official statistics are front-and-center", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "id": "c-statistical-api", "name": "Statistical APIs", "aliases": ["Statistical Application Programming Interfaces", "APIs"], "description": "Application programming interfaces exposing statistical data, enhanced with metadata and context.", "grounding_span": "Statistical Application Programming Interfaces (APIs) enhanced with metadata and context", "location": "Vision" },
    { "id": "c-metadata", "name": "Metadata", "aliases": ["high-quality metadata"], "description": "Descriptive context added to data access points to support accurate and trusted AI results.", "grounding_span": "APIs enhanced with metadata and context to support accurate and trusted generative AI results", "location": "Vision" },
    { "id": "c-fitness-for-use", "name": "Fitness for use", "aliases": [], "description": "The assessment of whether a data set is suitable for a given purpose.", "grounding_span": "to help analysts and the public assess fitness for use of data sets", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "id": "c-data-quality", "name": "Data quality", "aliases": [], "description": "The quality of data as evaluated across the FCSM Framework's domains and dimensions.", "grounding_span": "they need to be machine understandable and be able to support models that measure data quality", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "id": "c-contextual-info", "name": "Contextual information", "aliases": ["context"], "description": "Supporting context that models provide alongside data quality measurement.", "grounding_span": "support models that measure data quality and provide contextual information", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "id": "c-machine-understandable", "name": "Machine-understandable data", "aliases": ["machine understandability"], "description": "Data that AI can interpret by meaning, not merely parse by format; the target beyond machine-readable.", "grounding_span": "For statistical data to be accurately represented and trustworthy, they need to be machine understandable", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "id": "c-machine-readable", "name": "Machine-readable data", "aliases": [], "description": "Data published in machine-readable formats as required by open-data law; the baseline that AI-ready data goes beyond.", "grounding_span": "requires agencies to publish data in machine-readable formats", "location": "Approach" },
    { "id": "c-machine-interpretability", "name": "Machine interpretability", "aliases": [], "description": "The interpretability of public data by machines, subject of a cross-agency landscape analysis.", "grounding_span": "a cross-agency landscape analysis of “machine interpretability” of public data is already being explored", "location": "Roadmap step 1" },
    { "id": "c-semantic-meaning", "name": "Semantic meaning", "aliases": ["meaning of the data"], "description": "The meaning of data that must be preserved and considered beyond mere format.", "grounding_span": "while ensuring no semantic meaning is lost", "location": "Approach" },
    { "id": "c-accessibility", "name": "Accessibility", "aliases": [], "description": "A data quality dimension of the FCSM Framework.", "grounding_span": "meet the data quality dimensions of the Framework including, but not limited to, accessibility, timeliness, accuracy, and credibility", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "id": "c-timeliness", "name": "Timeliness", "aliases": [], "description": "A data quality dimension of the FCSM Framework.", "grounding_span": "accessibility, timeliness, accuracy, and credibility", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "id": "c-accuracy", "name": "Accuracy", "aliases": [], "description": "A data quality dimension of the FCSM Framework.", "grounding_span": "accessibility, timeliness, accuracy, and credibility", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "id": "c-credibility", "name": "Credibility", "aliases": [], "description": "A data quality dimension of the FCSM Framework.", "grounding_span": "accessibility, timeliness, accuracy, and credibility", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "id": "c-utility", "name": "Utility", "aliases": [], "description": "One of the three FCSM Framework domains for evaluating metadata improvements.", "grounding_span": "The FCSM framework provides three domains to evaluate these metadata improvements: Utility, Objectivity, and Integrity", "location": "Approach" },
    { "id": "c-objectivity", "name": "Objectivity", "aliases": [], "description": "One of the three FCSM Framework domains for evaluating metadata improvements.", "grounding_span": "The FCSM framework provides three domains to evaluate these metadata improvements: Utility, Objectivity, and Integrity", "location": "Approach" },
    { "id": "c-integrity", "name": "Integrity", "aliases": [], "description": "One of the three FCSM Framework domains for evaluating metadata improvements.", "grounding_span": "The FCSM framework provides three domains to evaluate these metadata improvements: Utility, Objectivity, and Integrity", "location": "Approach" },
    { "id": "c-ai-friendly-api", "name": "AI-friendly APIs", "aliases": ["standardized APIs", "AI-optimized APIs"], "description": "Standardized APIs designed for AI consumption of federal statistics.", "grounding_span": "exposing them through standardized, AI-friendly APIs", "location": "Approach" },
    { "id": "c-ai-ready-data", "name": "AI-ready data", "aliases": ["AI-ready federal statistical data"], "description": "Statistical data structured and documented so AI systems can discover, interpret, and correctly use it.", "grounding_span": "Creating AI-ready data builds on established open-data principles", "location": "Approach" },
    { "id": "c-authoritative-data", "name": "Authoritative data", "aliases": [], "description": "Official data whose prioritization in AI tools over nonauthoritative sources is a goal of AI-ready data guidance.", "grounding_span": "increase the prioritization of its authoritative data in AI tools over nonauthoritative sources", "location": "Approach" },
    { "id": "c-variable-level-metadata", "name": "Variable-level metadata", "aliases": [], "description": "Detailed context about each data element to support machine understandability.", "grounding_span": "in other words, giving AI detailed context about each data element", "location": "Approach" },
    { "id": "c-open-data-principles", "name": "Open-data principles", "aliases": [], "description": "Established open-data principles on which AI-ready data builds.", "grounding_span": "Creating AI-ready data builds on established open-data principles", "location": "Approach" },
    { "id": "c-data-dissemination", "name": "Data dissemination", "aliases": [], "description": "The widest, most accessible, appropriate dissemination of statistical products that agencies must strive for.", "grounding_span": "strive for the widest, most accessible, and appropriate dissemination of its statistical products", "location": "Impact & value" },
    { "id": "c-structured-experiments", "name": "Structured experiments", "aliases": ["pilots"], "description": "Empirical experiments to test how enhanced metadata and API standards affect AI-driven data access.", "grounding_span": "agencies can engage in structured experiments which build on the Commerce report", "location": "Roadmap" },
    { "id": "c-baseline-assessment", "name": "Baseline assessment", "aliases": ["baseline and follow-up assessments"], "description": "Evaluation of AI performance with existing data as a baseline before enhancements.", "grounding_span": "This provides a baseline of AI performance with the status quo", "location": "Roadmap step 1" },
    { "id": "c-hallucination", "name": "Hallucination", "aliases": [], "description": "Errors from poor models producing fabricated answers, a source of AI confusion.", "grounding_span": "poor models that hallucinate", "location": "Roadmap step 1" },
    { "id": "c-prompt-engineering", "name": "Prompt engineering", "aliases": [], "description": "Strategies AI tools use to consume data, contrasted with the judgments of analysts.", "grounding_span": "or prompt engineering", "location": "Roadmap step 1" },
    { "id": "c-ai-optimized-data-structures", "name": "AI-optimized data structures", "aliases": ["simplified JSON responses"], "description": "Data structures such as simplified JSON responses designed for easy AI parsing.", "grounding_span": "consider creating AI-optimized data structures", "location": "Roadmap step 2" },
    { "id": "c-summary-endpoints", "name": "Summary endpoints", "aliases": [], "description": "API endpoints an AI can call for quick contextual summaries of a dataset.", "grounding_span": "providing summary endpoints that an AI can call for quick", "location": "Roadmap step 2" },
    { "id": "c-integration-protocols", "name": "Integration protocols", "aliases": [], "description": "New protocols designed for AI to retrieve data in a controlled, standard way.", "grounding_span": "agencies should explore new integration protocols designed for AI", "location": "Roadmap step 2" },
    { "id": "c-cross-agency-learning", "name": "Cross-agency learning", "aliases": [], "description": "Documenting approaches and sharing lessons learned across agencies and partners.", "grounding_span": "3. Cross-Agency Learning: Statistical and other federal agencies", "location": "Roadmap step 3" },
    { "id": "c-ai-ready-working-group", "name": "AI-ready data working group", "aliases": [], "description": "A collaborative FCSM working group to review progress and refine best practices.", "grounding_span": "A collaborative “AI-ready data” working group for Federal agencies under FCSM", "location": "Roadmap step 3" },
    { "id": "c-toolkit", "name": "AI-readiness toolkit", "aliases": [], "description": "A practical toolkit for transforming statistical data products into AI-ready formats.", "grounding_span": "a practical toolkit for transforming statistical data products", "location": "Roadmap step 3" },
    { "id": "c-ranking-methods", "name": "Ranking methods for AI readiness", "aliases": [], "description": "Methods to rank the AI readiness of data, under active development.", "grounding_span": "efforts are already underway to develop such toolkits and ranking methods for AI readiness of data", "location": "Roadmap step 3" },
    { "id": "c-confidential-data", "name": "Confidential data", "aliases": [], "description": "Confidential data whose machine-understandability agencies may later explore, with added privacy considerations.", "grounding_span": "related to making confidential data more machine understandable", "location": "Roadmap step 3" },
    { "id": "c-privacy", "name": "Privacy considerations", "aliases": [], "description": "Additional privacy considerations arising when making confidential data machine-understandable.", "grounding_span": "and additional privacy considerations", "location": "Roadmap step 3" },
    { "id": "c-nsds", "name": "National Secure Data Service", "aliases": ["NSDS"], "description": "A project exploring a cross-agency landscape analysis of machine interpretability of public data.", "grounding_span": "already being explored through a National Secure Data Service (NSDS) project", "location": "Roadmap step 1" },
    { "id": "c-data-gov", "name": "data.gov catalogs", "aliases": [], "description": "Federal data catalogs that use the DCAT metadata standard.", "grounding_span": "the DCAT metadata standard used in data.gov catalogs", "location": "Roadmap step 2" }
  ],
  "definitions": [
    { "id": "def-mcp", "term": "Model Context Protocols (MCPs)", "verbatim_text": "Model Context Protocols (MCPs), open-source standard defined by https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": "2025-05", "grounding_span": "One promising example are Model Context Protocols (MCPs), open-source standard defined by https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol", "location": "Roadmap step 2" }
  ],
  "claims": [
    { "id": "cl1", "claim_text": "Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) is redefining how people interact with public information and shaping how public data are consumed.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) is redefining how people interact with public information and shaping how public data are consumed.", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "id": "cl2", "claim_text": "Recent advances in large language models mean that more Americans are getting answers from AI chatbots and other AI systems, which increasingly draw on public datasets.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) mean that \nmore Americans are getting answers from AI chatbots and other AI systems, which increasingly draw \non public datasets.", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "id": "cl3", "claim_text": "Generative AI acts as an intermediary in the consumption of public statistical information, extracting and combining data with logical strategies that differ from the thought processes and judgments of analysts.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Generative AI acts as an \nintermediary in the consumption of public statistical information, extracting and combining data with \nlogical strategies that differ from the thought processes and judgments of analysts.", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "id": "cl4", "claim_text": "For statistical data to be accurately represented and trustworthy, they need to be machine understandable and support models that measure data quality and provide contextual information.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "For statistical \ndata to be accurately represented and trustworthy, they need to be machine understandable and be \nable to support models that measure data quality and provide contextual information.", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "id": "cl5", "claim_text": "By enriching data access points with high-quality metadata and exposing them through standardized, AI-friendly APIs, agencies can significantly improve how LLMs discover, interpret, and relay publicly available federal statistics.", "claim_type": "speculative", "grounding_span": "By enriching data access points with high-quality metadata and exposing them through standardized, \nAI-friendly APIs, agencies can significantly improve how LLMs discover, interpret, and relay publicly \navailable federal statistics.", "location": "Approach" },
    { "id": "cl6", "claim_text": "This interpretation only considers the format of the data and does not consider the meaning of the data.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "However, this interpretation only considers the format of the data and does \nnot consider the meaning of the data.", "location": "Approach" },
    { "id": "cl7", "claim_text": "The FCSM framework provides three domains to evaluate these metadata improvements: Utility, Objectivity, and Integrity.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "The FCSM framework provides three domains to evaluate these metadata improvements: \nUtility, Objectivity, and Integrity.", "location": "Approach" },
    { "id": "cl8", "claim_text": "Title II of the OPEN Government Data Act already requires agencies to publish data in machine-readable formats while ensuring no semantic meaning is lost.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Title II of Public Law No: 115-435 \n(the OPEN Government Data Act) already requires agencies to publish data in machine-readable \nformats “while ensuring no semantic meaning is lost.”", "location": "Approach" },
    { "id": "cl9", "claim_text": "Today's AI era demands federal agencies go further – from machine-readable to machine-understandable data.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Today’s AI era demands federal agencies go \nfurther – from machine-readable to machine-understandable data.", "location": "Approach" },
    { "id": "cl10", "claim_text": "OMB Statistical Policy Directive 4 and 44 USC 3563(1)(a) instruct agencies to strive for the widest, most accessible, and appropriate dissemination of statistical products.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "OMB Statistical Policy Directive 4 and 44 USC 3563(1)(a) instructs agencies to strive for the widest, \nmost accessible, and appropriate dissemination of its statistical products.", "location": "Impact & value" },
    { "id": "cl11", "claim_text": "A cross-agency landscape analysis of machine interpretability of public data is already being explored through a National Secure Data Service project.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "a cross-\nagency landscape analysis of “machine interpretability” of public data is already being explored \nthrough a National Secure Data Service (NSDS) project.", "location": "Roadmap step 1" },
    { "id": "cl12", "claim_text": "The Department of Commerce's recent AI-ready data initiative exemplifies this approach.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "The Department of Commerce’s recent “AI-ready data” initiative \nexemplifies this approach.", "location": "Approach" },
    { "id": "cl13", "claim_text": "Creating AI-ready data builds on established open-data principles.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Creating AI-ready data builds on established open-data principles.", "location": "Approach" },
    { "id": "cl14", "claim_text": "Efforts are already underway to develop toolkits and ranking methods for AI readiness of data.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "efforts are already \nunderway to develop such toolkits and ranking methods for AI readiness of data", "location": "Roadmap step 3" }
  ],

    { "type": "mentions", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "c-ai-ready-working-group", "grounding_span": "A collaborative “AI-ready data” working group for Federal agencies under FCSM", "location": "Roadmap step 3" },
    { "type": "mentions", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "c-toolkit", "grounding_span": "a practical toolkit for transforming statistical data products", "location": "Roadmap step 3" },
    { "type": "mentions", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "c-ranking-methods", "grounding_span": "ranking methods for AI readiness of data", "location": "Roadmap step 3" },
    { "type": "mentions", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "c-confidential-data", "grounding_span": "related to making confidential data more machine understandable", "location": "Roadmap step 3" },
    { "type": "mentions", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "c-privacy", "grounding_span": "and additional privacy \nconsiderations", "location": "Roadmap step 3" },
    { "type": "mentions", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "c-nsds", "grounding_span": "already being explored \nthrough a National Secure Data Service (NSDS) project", "location": "Roadmap step 1" },
    { "type": "mentions", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "c-data-gov", "grounding_span": "the DCAT metadata standard used in data.gov catalogs", "location": "Roadmap step 2" },

    { "type": "defines", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "def-mcp", "grounding_span": "One promising example are Model Context Protocols (MCPs), open-source standard defined \nby https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol", "location": "Roadmap step 2" },

    { "type": "asserts", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "cl1", "grounding_span": "Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) is redefining how people interact with public information and \nshaping how public data are consumed.", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "type": "asserts", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "cl2", "grounding_span": "Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) mean that \nmore Americans are getting answers from AI chatbots and other AI systems", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "type": "asserts", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "cl3", "grounding_span": "Generative AI acts as an \nintermediary in the consumption of public statistical information", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "type": "asserts", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "cl4", "grounding_span": "For statistical \ndata to be accurately represented and trustworthy, they need to be machine understandable", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity" },
    { "type": "asserts", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "cl5", "grounding_span": "By enriching data access points with high-quality metadata and exposing them through standardized, \nAI-friendly APIs, agencies can significantly improve how LLMs discover, interpret, and relay publicly \navailable federal statistics.", "location": "Approach" },
    { "type": "asserts", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "cl6", "grounding_span": "However, this interpretation only considers the format of the data and does \nnot consider the meaning of the data.", "location": "Approach" },
    { "type": "asserts", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "cl7", "grounding_span": "The FCSM framework provides three domains to evaluate these metadata improvements: \nUtility, Objectivity, and Integrity.", "location": "Approach" },
    { "type": "asserts", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "cl8", "grounding_span": "Title II of Public Law No: 115-435 \n(the OPEN Government Data Act) already requires agencies to publish data in machine-readable \nformats “while ensuring no semantic meaning is lost.”", "location": "Approach" },
    { "type": "asserts", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "cl9", "grounding_span": "Today’s AI era demands federal agencies go \nfurther – from machine-readable to machine-understandable data.", "location": "Approach" },
    { "type": "asserts", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "cl10", "grounding_span": "OMB Statistical Policy Directive 4 and 44 USC 3563(1)(a) instructs agencies to strive for the widest, \nmost accessible, and appropriate dissemination of its statistical products.", "location": "Impact & value" },
    { "type": "asserts", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "cl11", "grounding_span": "a cross-\nagency landscape analysis of “machine interpretability” of public data is already being explored \nthrough a National Secure Data Service (NSDS) project.", "location": "Roadmap step 1" },
    { "type": "asserts", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "cl12", "grounding_span": "The Department of Commerce’s recent “AI-ready data” initiative \nexemplifies this approach.", "location": "Approach" },
    { "type": "asserts", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "cl13", "grounding_span": "Creating AI-ready data builds on established open-data principles.", "location": "Approach" },
    { "type": "asserts", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "cl14", "grounding_span": "efforts are already \nunderway to develop such toolkits and ranking methods for AI readiness of data", "location": "Roadmap step 3" },

    { "type": "cites", "from_id": "fcsm-25-03", "to_id": "doc-commerce-report", "grounding_span": "One guideline from the Commerce report recommends adding “comprehensive variable-level", "location": "Approach" }

  "proposed_relationships": [
    { "grounding_span": "AI-based queries present \nnew challenges, and the framework should be enhanced to meet them.", "suggested_edge": "enhances", "location": "Challenge & Opportunity", "note": "The document proposes enhancing/extending the FCSM Framework for Data Quality to meet AI-based query challenges — a framework-extension not expressible as Framework→Framework since only one framework node exists." },
    { "grounding_span": "Creating AI-ready data builds on established open-data principles.", "suggested_edge": "builds_on", "location": "Approach", "note": "Concept→Concept builds_on (AI-ready data builds on open-data principles); schema restricts builds_on to Standard/Framework→Standard/Framework." },
    { "grounding_span": "Title II of Public Law No: 115-435 \n(the OPEN Government Data Act) already requires agencies to publish data in machine-readable \nformats", "suggested_edge": "requires", "location": "Approach", "note": "Statutory requirement from the OPEN Government Data Act onto machine-readable formats; no statute→concept requirement edge exists." },
    { "grounding_span": "One promising example are Model Context Protocols (MCPs), open-source standard defined", "suggested_edge": "example_of", "location": "Roadmap step 2", "note": "MCPs are offered as an example of the 'new integration protocols designed for AI' concept; is-a/example relation between a Standard and a Concept is not cleanly expressible." },
    { "grounding_span": "a cross-\nagency landscape analysis of “machine interpretability” of public data is already being explored \nthrough a National Secure Data Service (NSDS) project", "suggested_edge": "explores", "location": "Roadmap step 1", "note": "The NSDS project explores machine interpretability of public data — an organization/project→activity relationship not in the edge vocabulary." },
    { "grounding_span": "Use \ncommon schemas or standards like the DCAT metadata standard used in data.gov catalogs", "suggested_edge": "used_by", "location": "Roadmap step 2", "note": "DCAT (Standard) is used by data.gov catalogs (Concept/resource); no Standard↔resource usage edge exists." }
  ]
}
